summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the SI unit of magnetic field?

A magnetic force acts on moving charges: F = qv × B. The direction follows the right-hand rule. Current-carrying wires create magnetic fields and experience forces in external fields. The answer is 'Tesla (T)'. The other options — 'Newton (N)', 'Henry (H)', and 'Weber (Wb)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is the force on a moving charge in a B field?

Coulomb's Law: F = kq₁q₂/r², where k = 8.99×10⁹ N·m²/C². Like charges repel; opposite charges attract. Force varies inversely with distance squared. The answer is 'F = qvB sin θ'. The other options — 'F = qvB cos θ', 'F = qB/v', and 'F = qv/B' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
